blurb: Apple platforms implement IPv6 privacy extensions per RFC 4941, generating semantically opaque randomized Interface Identifiers for SLAAC-configured addresses rather than deriving them from hardware MAC addresses. These privacy addresses rotate periodically and upon joining new networks, preventing correlation of a device's network activity across time or location by passive observers. iOS and iPadOS use separate independent IPv6 addresses for different network services to limit cross-service correlation, and the system prefers iCloud Private Relay paths where available to reduce exposure of the device's real IP address to network observers and servers.
